title: How to Build Your Own Table When There's No Seat for You
guestBio: Neetu Sadhwani is an entrepreneur and business leader who exemplifies the courage to leave comfort behind in pursuit of purpose. She's built her career by creating opportunities rather than waiting for them, demonstrating the power of building your own table when existing structures don't make room for you.
excerpt: In this premiere episode, Neetu Sadhwani shares her journey from comfort zone to purpose-driven entrepreneur. Get ready for real talk on burnout, building your own opportunities, and why waiting for permission is overrated.
metaDescription: Neetu Sadhwani shares how she left comfort behind to build her own table. Real talk on burnout, bold moves, and believing in yourself as a woman leader.
keyTakeaways: Building your own table is more powerful than waiting for a seat at someone else's.
Leaving comfort behind requires courage, but purpose-driven work is worth the risk.
Burnout is a signal that you need to reassess your boundaries and priorities.
Believing in yourself is a practice that requires consistent nurturing, especially during difficult times.
Grace and grit can coexist in your leadership journey.
Creating opportunities for yourself opens doors for other women to follow.
Your vision matters more than external validation or permission.

pullQuotes.text: Why waiting for a seat at the table just isn't it anymore — she built her own

content:
Welcome to the very first episode of The Manage Her podcast, where we're redefining what it means to lead as women in a world that wasn't always designed for us. I'm your host, Aimee Rickabus, and I couldn't be more excited to kick things off with the incredible Neetu Sadhwani — a woman who embodies everything this podcast stands for.

If you've ever felt stuck waiting for someone else to recognize your worth, if you've questioned whether you have what it takes to build something meaningful, or if you've wrestled with that nagging voice that tells you to play it safe, this conversation is for you. Neetu's story is a masterclass in what happens when you stop asking for permission and start creating your own opportunities.

The Courage to Leave Comfort Behind

One of the most powerful themes in our conversation is Neetu's decision to leave comfort behind in pursuit of something greater. This isn't about being reckless or impulsive — it's about recognizing when your current situation, no matter how secure it feels, isn't aligned with who you're becoming or what you're called to create.

Neetu shares the pivotal moments when she realized that staying comfortable was actually the riskier choice. While everyone around her saw security and stability, she saw limitation and a slow death of her potential. The courage to leave comfort isn't a one-time decision; it's a series of daily choices to prioritize growth over safety, purpose over predictability.

What strikes me most about Neetu's approach is how she balanced this boldness with wisdom. She didn't just leap without looking — she prepared, she planned, and she trusted herself enough to bet on her own vision. That's the kind of strategic courage we need more of as women leaders.

Building Your Own Table

Perhaps the most game-changing insight from our conversation is Neetu's philosophy around building your own table rather than waiting for a seat at someone else's. This isn't just a catchy phrase — it's a fundamental shift in how we approach opportunity and leadership.

For too long, women have been told to work twice as hard to earn half the recognition, to wait patiently for our turn, to be grateful for whatever scraps of opportunity come our way. Neetu's story demolishes that narrative completely. She shows us what becomes possible when we stop asking for permission and start creating the exact opportunities we want to see.

Building your own table means defining success on your own terms. It means creating the kind of workplace culture you want to be part of, pursuing the projects that light you up, and designing a career that serves your values rather than compromising them. When you build your own table, you're not just creating opportunities for yourself — you're creating space for other women to step into their power too.

The Real Talk on Burnout

Let's be honest — the path of building something meaningful isn't always glamorous. Neetu doesn't shy away from the harder truths about entrepreneurship and leadership, including the very real challenge of burnout. What I love about her perspective is that she doesn't treat burnout as a badge of honor or an inevitable part of success.

Instead, she frames burnout as valuable information — a signal that something in your system needs to change. Maybe it's your boundaries, maybe it's your priorities, or maybe it's the way you're trying to do everything yourself instead of building the support you need. Burnout isn't a character flaw; it's feedback.

Neetu's approach to managing this reality is both practical and compassionate. She talks about the importance of building sustainability into your vision from the beginning, rather than trying to retrofit balance into an unsustainable situation. This is about designing a life and career that can go the distance, not just sprint to the next milestone.

Believing in Yourself When It's Hard

One of the most relatable parts of our conversation is Neetu's honesty about the ongoing work of believing in yourself. This isn't a one-and-done mindset shift — it's a daily practice, especially when you're doing something that's never been done before or blazing a trail in uncharted territory.

Neetu shares practical strategies for nurturing self-belief, even when external circumstances might suggest otherwise. She talks about the importance of celebrating small wins, surrounding yourself with people who see your vision even when you can't, and developing the kind of internal compass that keeps you oriented toward your goals even when the path gets murky.

What resonates most is her acknowledgment that believing in yourself doesn't mean never having doubts. It means having doubts and choosing to move forward anyway. It means treating uncertainty as part of the process, not evidence that you're not cut out for what you're trying to build.

Grace, Grit, and Everything In Between

Throughout our conversation, what emerges is a picture of leadership that doesn't require you to choose between strength and softness, ambition and authenticity, success and soul. Neetu embodies what I call integrated leadership — the ability to bring your whole self to your work while still driving results and creating impact.

This integration isn't just nice to have; it's essential for sustainable success. When you can lead with both grace and grit, when you can be both visionary and practical, when you can hold space for both your ambitions and your humanity, you create the kind of leadership that doesn't just achieve goals — it transforms the people and communities around you.

Neetu's story is proof that you don't have to sacrifice who you are to become who you're meant to be. In fact, the most powerful version of your leadership is likely the one that honors all the different facets of your identity and experience.

This conversation sets the tone for everything we're building with The Manage Her. We're here for the real talk, the honest conversations, and the reminder that you're more capable than you think. We're here to challenge the narratives that keep women small and to celebrate the ones who are bold enough to rewrite the rules.

Your vision matters. Your voice matters. Your leadership matters. And if there's no table that fits who you're becoming, it's time to build your own.
